meta.title: Ship or Die — Ship a startup in 30 days, or get kicked out forever
meta.description: Ship a startup in 30 days, or get kicked out forever. A private crew for people forced to launch.
meta.og_title: Ship or Die — Ship a startup in 30 days, or get kicked out forever
meta.og_description: Ship a startup in 30 days, or get kicked out forever. A private crew for people forced to launch.

headline_parts: miss the deadline, get kicked out forever.

problem.layout: Sticky scroll-reveal text, min-h 145svh, pixel noise texture overlay

copy_sequence.text: Not because the idea is bad...
...but because nobody can actually use your app yet.
You've been two weeks away for four months 💀

how_it_works.headline: Ship an app every 30 days until one changes your life

steps.label: step
steps.title: Become a pirate
steps.intro: You are the average of the five people you spend the most time with. Join a private crew of builders who ship, not productivity tourists.
bullets: Join our Discord community of pirates who ship
Hold each other accountable with daily check-ins
Feeling solo? Join the voice channels and cowork with others

steps.label: step
steps.title: Make small bets
steps.intro: Building a business is scary. Build one feature with a buy button instead. We break down the core of making small bets into a gamified startup roadmap.
bullets: One feature. A buy button. That's all you need to validate
Overthinking? Each step guides you to the next
Finally launch your idea — it's mandatory

steps.label: step
steps.title: Don't quit
steps.intro: This is the whole game. Ship or Die exists so quitting feels stupid and shipping feels fun. Keep building until you find the startup that changes your life.
bullets: Public mission profiles that keep you accountable
30-day deadlines and trophies that turn shipping into a game
Ship, launch, repeat is your new normal

consequences.headline: Miss the deadline...
penalties: Hall of shame in the public Discord channel
consequences.visual: Plank walk animation — pirate walks plank, falls, shark

people.name: Marc Lou
people.bio: Marc thought he was Mark Zuckerberg and spent 2 years building ambitious startups nobody wanted: 0 users, $0 revenue. So he changed strategy: build, launch, repeat. He has now launched 30+ startups, including DataFast ($22K MRR) and TrustMRR ($33K MRR).

people.name: jack friks
people.bio: Jack quit McDonald's to learn to code in his mom's basement. His first app Curiosity Quench hit 100,000 downloads, then he shipped Post Bridge (now $35K MRR) and Lovelee Couples (100,000+ downloads).

pricing.headline: Join the crew.
includes: Public profile with all your apps
pricing.warning: No refund. Ship or get kicked out.

items.question: What app can I work on?+
items.answer: Anything, as long as it can be shared with a link. SaaS, tool, directory, marketplace, AI wrapper, cursed little experiment. If the crew can click it, it counts.

items.question: What if I already have an idea?+
items.answer: Good. Bring it aboard and stop polishing the treasure map. The point is to ship the smallest version people can actually use.

items.question: What if I do not have an idea yet?+
items.answer: You will pick one fast. No hiding in the fog for three weeks asking the wind for permission.

items.question: Do I need to be a technical founder?+
items.answer: No. You need enough grit to get a link live. Code it, vibe code it, duct-tape it. The sea does not care how the boat floats.

items.question: When does the 30-day timer start?+
items.answer: After checkout, you choose the project you are committing to. Once that mission is created, the 30-day timer starts. After you ship and stay on board, you do not have to start another mission right away. Keep working on your startup if it is taking off. Start a new mission whenever you want, and that starts a new 30-day timer.

items.question: What happens if I do not launch in 30 days?+
items.answer: You get marked overboard, lose community access, and become a warning tale for the next batch of pirates who thought they were special.

items.question: Can I change my idea after joining?+
items.answer: Yes, early and for a real reason. But once you've signed the oath, you're committed to the idea and the 30-day clock.

items.question: Is this a course?+
items.answer: No. Courses let you feel productive while staying hidden. This is a deadline, an exclusive community, and a public launch waiting to judge your excuses.

items.question: How much time do I need each week?+
items.answer: Enough to move the ship daily. Some pirates have full-time jobs, kids, chaos, and bad Wi-Fi. The rule is progress, not perfect conditions.

items.question: Will people help me?+
items.answer: Aye. You get the crew, feedback, pressure, and visibility. You still have to swing the hammer yourself.

items.question: Can I get a refund?+
items.answer: If you genuinely asked this, blink twice and start over.
